direction: Following bar graph shows the sales of books (in thousand number) from Five branches of a publishing company during two consecutive years 2010 and 2011.
content: What is the ratio of total sale of branch $B_2$ for both year to the total sale of branch $B_4$ for both year?
explanation:
$\frac{\text { Total sale of Branch } B_2}{\text { Total sale of Branch } B_4}=\frac{75+65}{85+95}$

Required ratio $=\frac{140}{180}=\frac{7}{9}$

direction: Following bar graph shows the sales of books (in thousand number) from Five branches of a publishing company during two consecutive years 2010 and 2011.
content: What is the average sales of all the branches (in thousand numbers) for the year of 2010?
explanation:
According to the question, Average sales of all the five branches (in thousand number) for the year 2010

$$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{80+75+95+85+75}{5} \\ & =\frac{410}{5}=82 \end{aligned}$$

direction: Following bar graph shows the income and expenditure (in ₹ lakhs) of a company. Read the graph carefully and answer the questions.
content: What is the average income of the company over the years?
explanation: Average income of the company over the years $$=\frac{30+50+40+60+60}{5}$$ lakhs $$=\frac{240}{5}$$ lakhs = ₹ 48 lakhs

direction: Following bar graph shows the income and expenditure (in ₹ lakhs) of a company. Read the graph carefully and answer the questions.
content: Which of the following pairs of years, shows the equal expenditure of the company?
explanation: From the bar graph, it is clear that in 1983 and 1984 the expenditure of the company is same.

direction: Study the following graph and answer the questions.
content: If the imports of Company X in 2007 were increased by $$40 \%$$, what would be the ratio of exports to the increased imports?
explanation: $$\begin{aligned} & \text { In 2007, for Company X, } \\ & \frac{\text { Exports }}{\text { Imports }}=1.75 \\ & \text { Let exports }=1.75 a \\ & \text { and } \quad \text { imports }=a \\ & \text { New imports }=\left(\frac{100+40}{100}\right) \times a=\frac{140}{100} a \\ & \therefore \text { Required ratio }=\frac{\text { Exports }}{\text { New imports }}=\frac{1.75 a}{\frac{140}{100} a} \\ & =\frac{175}{140}=1.25 \\ \end{aligned}$$

direction: Study the following graph and answer the questions.
content: In 2005, the exports of Company $$\mathrm{X}$$ were doubled that of Company Y in that year. If the imports of Company $$\mathrm{X}$$ during the year were ₹ ~180 crore, what was the amount (in ₹ crore) of imports of Company Y during the year?
explanation:
Given, imports of Company X in

$$2005=$$₹ 1$$80 \text { crore }$$

In 2005, for Company $$X$$,

$$\frac{\text { Exports }}{\text { Imports }}=1.75$$

$$\therefore$$ Exports of Company $$\mathrm{X}$$ in 2005

$$\begin{aligned} & =(1.75 \times 180) \text { crore } \\\\ & = ~315 \text { crore } \end{aligned}$$

or exports of Company Y in 2005

$$=\left(\frac{ ~315}{2}\right) \text { crore }$$

$$=$$₹ $$157.5$$ crore

Also, in 2005, for Company Y,

$$\frac{\text { Exports }}{\text { Imports }}=0.75$$

Hence, imports of Company Y in 2005

$$\begin{aligned} & =\left(\frac{157.5}{0.75}\right) \text { crore } \\\\ & = ~210 \text { crore } \end{aligned}$$
